message:
fix(core): respect huge_file flag in block accounting and add COW for shared xattr blocks

Block count accounting:
When the EXT4_HUGE_FILE_FL flag is set on an inode, the i_blocks field
is counted in filesystem blocks rather than 512-byte sectors. All paths
that adjust inode.blocks (mkdir, directory expansion, write allocation,
truncation/free, symlink block allocation) now check is_huge_file() and
use the correct unit, preventing inflated block counts on large
filesystems that enable the huge_file feature.

Xattr external block COW:
The set_xattr and remove_xattr paths now read the xattr block header's
refcount field (bytes 4..8 of the external block). When an xattr block
is shared (refcount > 1), mutating it performs copy-on-write: a new
block is allocated, the modified payload is written there, and the old
block's refcount is decremented. When removing xattrs empties a shared
block, only the refcount is decremented instead of freeing. When
allocating a fresh xattr block in set_xattr, inode.blocks is now
properly incremented (accounting for the huge_file unit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
